items.text: A one-to-two-order-of-magnitude launch-cost collapse changes the binding question from destination to downstream economic beneficiaries.
The EV analogy points to inputs and picks-and-shovels rather than only the headline vehicle maker.
Cheap launch can make previously uneconomic payload classes viable, including orbital compute, in-space services, and space-grade component supply.

excluded.reason: target case solution material withheld from retrieval
